source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle. Point E is the midpoint of AC. A line through point E is perpendicular to AB at point F. The line BC is extended to intersect the extension of EF at point D. If EF = 1, what is the length of DF?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Connect BE, \\
since $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle and point E is the midpoint of AC, \\
it follows that $\angle ABC=60^\circ$, $\angle ABE=\angle CBE=30^\circ$, \\
since EF$\perp$AB, \\
it follows that $\angle D=90^\circ-\angle ABC=30^\circ$, that is, $\angle D=\angle CBE=30^\circ$, \\
thus BE=DE, \\
in $\triangle BEF$, EF=1, \\
hence BE=2EF=2, \\
thus BE=DE=2, \\
therefore DF=EF+DE=\boxed{3},
